page: OVHcloud rtx5000-84 is a dedicated GPU-accelerated server instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon W-3235 processor with 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs operating at a base frequency of 3.3 GHz. The CPU architecture is x86_64, supporting advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX-512 Foundation, and AVX-512 Vector Neural Network Instructions. The system is equipped with 84.0 GB of system memory, providing 5.25 GB per core, alongside a local 400 GB SSD storage volume and a 10 Gbps baseline network interface.

The primary performance driver of this instance is its triple-GPU configuration, featuring three NVIDIA Quadro RTX 5000 GPUs based on the Turing architecture. This setup delivers a combined 48 GB of VRAM, with 16 GB allocated per GPU. In benchmark testing, this hardware configuration achieves top-tier performance in large language model inference tasks. Prompt processing speeds reach 8,884.89 tokens per second for a small 135M model and 220.48 tokens per second for a large 70B model, while text generation speeds range from 332.27 tokens per second to 7.46 tokens per second for the respective models.

General compute and memory benchmarks place the instance in the average to strong tiers. The Passmark memory score is strong, supported by memory bandwidth speeds of up to 990.44 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 45.28 GB/sec for large uncached blocks. CPU-bound benchmarks, such as Geekbench multi-core and Passmark CPU, fall within the average range. However, text processing performance is a relative bottleneck, scoring in the bottom 25% on Geekbench. Local storage and networking support steady throughput, with static web serving reaching 11.46 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the rtx5000-84 offers a dense allocation of three physical GPUs and dedicated, non-hyperthreaded Intel Xeon cores, making it a highly specialized resource for parallel processing without the overhead of shared-core environments. This configuration is highly suited for GPU-accelerated workloads, including LLM inference, machine learning model training, 3D rendering, and parallelized scientific simulations. It is also well-suited for database operations and multi-threaded decompression tasks that can leverage the dedicated CPU cores and high memory bandwidth.
description: OVHcloud rtx5000-84 is a dedicated GPU-accelerated server instance featuring an Intel Xeon W-3235 processor with 16 physical cores and 16 vCPUs running at 3.3 GHz. It is equipped with 84.0 GB of system RAM, a 400 GB local SSD, and a 10 Gbps network interface. The defining feature of this instance is its triple NVIDIA Quadro RTX 5000 GPU setup, providing a total of 48 GB of VRAM. In performance evaluations, the instance delivers top-tier results in LLM inference, achieving 220.48 tokens per second for prompt processing on a 70B model. While general CPU benchmarks like Geekbench multi-core and Passmark CPU score in the average range, the strong memory bandwidth and dedicated GPU resources make this instance highly efficient for parallel computing. It is optimized for machine learning inference, 3D rendering, and data science workloads.
og_description: OVHcloud rtx5000-84 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 84 GB RAM, a 400 GB SSD, and three NVIDIA Quadro RTX 5000 GPUs providing 48 GB of total VRAM.
meta_description: OVHcloud rtx5000-84 is a dedicated GPU server with 16 Intel Xeon cores, 84 GB RAM, and three NVIDIA Quadro RTX 5000 GPUs for parallel workloads.
tagline: A dedicated multi-GPU server featuring three hardware accelerators and high-bandwidth memory for demanding parallel computing and machine learning workloads.
bullet_points: Three NVIDIA Quadro RTX 5000 GPUs providing 48 GB of total VRAM
16 dedicated Intel Xeon W-3235 physical cores operating at 3.3 GHz
84 GB of system memory with strong read/write bandwidth performance
Top-tier benchmark performance for small, medium, and large LLM inference
400 GB local SSD storage and a 10 Gbps baseline network interface
Best suited for machine learning, 3D rendering, and parallel data processing
